title: Cluster Networking, CNI, NetworkPolicy, Dual Stack, and Layered Troubleshooting · Cluster Case: K8s Cni Cluster Network Model
summary: A multi-tier application has working DNS but failed connections, an additive policy gap, asymmetric dual-stack behavior, and a provider-specific data plane. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s cni cluster network model increment in network intent and isolation evidence policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace Pod addressing, routing, CNI responsibilities, Service implementation, node paths, NAT, MTU, and platform-specific exceptions.
Authorize cross-namespace references with ReferenceGrant and verify Accepted, Programmed, ResolvedRefs, observedGeneration, attached routes, and implementation conformance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster

steps.title: Predict K8s Cni Cluster Network Model · Theory K8s Cni Cluster Network Model
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Pod can resolve DNS but cannot reach a backend after default deny; the policy selects only ingress, misses egress DNS, and assumes NetworkPolicy implementation behavior. Trace source, destination, port, direction, selector, CNI, and node boundary. Competency probe: Trace Pod addressing, routing, CNI responsibilities, Service implementation, node paths, NAT, MTU, and platform-specific exceptions.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Pod can resolve DNS but cannot reach a backend after default deny; the policy selects only ingress, misses egress DNS, and assumes NetworkPolicy implementation behavior. Trace source, destination, port, direction, selector, CNI, and node boundary. Competency probe: Trace Pod addressing, routing, CNI responsibilities, Service implementation, node paths, NAT, MTU, and platform-specific exceptions. A defensible solution applies K8s Cni Cluster Network Model, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to network intent and isolation evidence policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-network-model-policy-troubleshooting-5
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-network-model-policy-troubleshooting-5
    artifact: network-policy.yaml
    decision: worked k8s network model policy troubleshooting 5
    environment: managed canary cluster with provider-specific load balancing
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      pod-network: cni-implementation-boundary
      ip-family-policy: PreferDualStack
      ingress: default-deny-plus-gateway
      egress: default-deny-plus-dns-and-api
      metadata-endpoint: denied
      return-path: stateful-implementation-verified
    verify: kubectl get networkpolicy,pod,service,endpointslice -n intake -o json
    failure: Reject policy override semantics, automatic return-path claims, DNS-success as endpoint proof, or a CNI-independent packet conclusion.
    repair: Derive additive ingress and egress allows, preserve DNS and required control paths, then test name, endpoint, route, policy, and application layers.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: The Kubernetes API specification installs and configures one universal network data plane. This fails the K8s Cni Cluster Network Model contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
